descriptionText: Catamaran CK 64 for sale 2019 The CK64 is a 64-foot long aluminum catamaran that blends elegance with robust engineering. This model features a spacious design that includes four comfortable double cabins for guests and two additional cabins for the crew, making it suitable for luxury cruises or charter services. Key Specifications: - Length of the hull: 19.60 meters - Overall beam (width): 9.20 meters - Draft: 1.40 meters - Displacement: 32 tons - Power options include twin 110 HP engines as standard, with the option to upgrade to 140 HP turbo engines. - The fuel capacity is substantial, holding up to 2 x 3,200 liters of diesel, ensuring long-range cruising capabilities. - Freshwater storage is also ample with two tanks, each carrying 1,200 liters, ideal for extended voyages. - For convenience and environmental considerations, the catamaran comes equipped with a high-density polyethylene (HDPE) system for black water with a dedicated nacelle, and a VETUS system for gray water management in the hull bottoms, each fitted with an individual lift pump. - Additionally, the CK64 holds two 80-liter and two 50-liter tanks for black and gray water, respectively. Designed with both performance and comfort in mind, the CK64 catamaran is a premium choice for those desiring a blend of luxury and practicality in marine travel. Hull and Deck Construction: - Hulls and superstructures constructed from aluminum alloy 5083 H111 - MIG-welded lined sheets with penetrant testing for integrity - Alternately welded stringers, ribs, and floors - Watertight bulkheads welded on both sides - Integrated diesel and water tanks - Non-ground welds on non-visible surfaces enhance weld resistance and nacelle safety Appendages and Bar Systems: - Profiled aluminum rudders - Aluminum rudder stocks - Marsili hydraulic tiller system - Aluminum quillons Insulation: - Thermal and sound insulation using projected polyurethane foam above the waterline - Engine and generator compartments feature reinforced insulation with rock wool, sound insulation foam, and perforated aluminum sheet Paint Coatings and Finishes: - International/awl grip mixed process - Underwater parts: degreasing, pickling, 3-4 coats of epoxy primer totaling 200 μ, 1 coat of 50 μ epoxy vinyl, 2 coats of antifouling - Topsides: degreasing, pickling, 50 μ epoxy primer, epoxy filler, polyurethane undercoat, and 3 polyester urethane finish coats - Anti-slip epoxy paint on decks and traffic areas - Teak steps on the rear cockpit, flybridge, and skirts, 8mm thick Mast and Boom: - 24m painted aluminum mast, Maréchal Z 1602 - 8.50m painted aluminum boom - Navigation and anchor LED lights, wind vane, topmast light, and deck light - Retractable mast rungs - Ladder for coachroof access via forward cockpit on starboard - Aluminum bowsprit Standing and Furling Rigging: - Sparcraft monostrand stainless steel rigging - Profurl NC530 electric genoa furler - Fixed staysail with carabiner Sails: - Elvstrom cruise dacron double ply supplier - Fully battened mainsail of 120m² with 3 reefs and anti-chafe strips - 130m² genoa with UV-protection band - Total sail area of 250m² upwind and 320m² downwind Safety and Protection: - Stainless steel aft and bow pulpits and Ø26 stanchions - Handrail behind aft cockpit seats - Lifeline anchors in cockpits and on deck - Welded aluminum toe rail - 4mm double-level stainless steel die - 85cm high chandeliers and balconies - Wooden seats on front pulpit - A door on each side Cockpit and Aft Skirts: - A vast aft cockpit shielded by the upper deck and equipped with lighting - Seating with large storage compartments - Teak flooring and steps - Three benches featuring storage boxes - Six combination stool/storage units - Drink refrigerator and freezer - Cockpit dining table - Comfortable stainless steel swim ladder on the port skirt for man overboard situations - Customizable aft cockpit upholstery for seats and backrests - Customizable aft cockpit sundeck upholstery - Complete aft cockpit enclosure - Underwater lighting with high-intensity projectors at the stern Helm Station: - Elevated helm station with a two-seater pilot seat - Helming console equipped with a magnetic compass - Console with cable-operated engine controls - Raymarine Axiom 15 navigation unit, visible from the helm - Composite steering wheel - Electronic engine controls - Remote control for engine and navigation systems for docking and anchoring Main Features: - Interior finishes made of veneered marine plywood sandwich and honeycomb (using NIDAPLAST with formica and glued edges on visible parts) for weight reduction - Bathrooms finished in white laminate - Kitchen countertops made of synthetic stone, akin to Corian - Headroom of 2.15-2.25m in living quarters - Safety handrails in descent areas - Marine grade hardware - Indirect and step lighting for ambiance and safety Galley: - U-shaped galley on starboard side with access to the aft cockpit via sliding window - Double deep stainless steel sink with hot/cold water tap - Synthetic stone counters - Storage in cupboards and a storage column - Professional 3-burner gas cooker (Burgundy Embassy) - External venting extractor hood - Large HAIER HB21F refrigerator/freezer, or similar model - Dishwasher Cabins: - Four "Grand Comfort" cabins - 150 x 200 cm double beds with dual-side access in aft cabins - High-density mattresses on slatted bases - Ample storage including large wardrobes, shelves, and drawers - Office space, LED and ambient lighting, with two reading lights in each cabin - 2.15 m headroom, large windows, deck hatch, and large mirror in each cabin Bathrooms: - One bathroom per cabin - PLANUS electric toilet - Basin set in counter with storage and hot/cold mixer tap - Towel rack, mirror with LED lighting - Port window in the hull, large deck hatch, and large windows - Separate shower with plexiglass walls Machinery: - Twin 110 HP engines, optional SD4.140 TIC SCAM 3.8l - Sea water filter, electric motor control - Double cylinder hydraulic transmission Marsili - Forced air engine compartment ventilation - SCAN SDA10M 10Kva 380V generator and 280l/h Hydrotechnical watermaker Electricity: - 24V electrical system with a set of 1,600 Ah lead gel service batteries - 4 x 140A lithium batteries - 24V main panel with battery monitor, protection box - Dual 190Ah alternators for lithium batteries - Engine starter batteries with redundancy switch - Numerous AC outlets and USB sockets throughout the vessel - LED lighting both outside and inside, ambient, courtesy, and underwater lights Onboard Comfort: - Washer-dryer in starboard cockpit, extra cockpit refrigeration or storage - Individual U Flex velair air conditioning 7000 BTU per cabin and compact systems in saloon - Optional fuel heating Sound and Electronics: - Raymarine primary electronics supplier - VHF with DSC, AIS700, i70s pack, AXIOM 12' touch screens, and wireless remote control - Quantum Q24D Doppler radar, Realvision 3D probe, and backup autopilot system - Marine stereo with Bluetooth, interior and exterior loudspeakers, radio pack - Customized audio-video system in the saloon
descriptionHtml:
Catamaran CK 64 for sale 2019
The CK64 is a 64-foot long aluminum catamaran that blends elegance with robust engineering. This model features a spacious design that includes four comfortable double cabins for guests and two additional cabins for the crew, making it suitable for luxury cruises or charter services.
Key Specifications:
- Length of the hull: 19.60 meters
- Overall beam (width): 9.20 meters
- Draft: 1.40 meters
- Displacement: 32 tons
- Power options include twin 110 HP engines as standard, with the option to upgrade to 140 HP turbo engines.
- The fuel capacity is substantial, holding up to 2 x 3,200 liters of diesel, ensuring long-range cruising capabilities.
- Freshwater storage is also ample with two tanks, each carrying 1,200 liters, ideal for extended voyages.
- For convenience and environmental considerations, the catamaran comes equipped with a high-density polyethylene (HDPE) system for black water with a dedicated nacelle, and a VETUS system for gray water management in the hull bottoms, each fitted with an individual lift pump.
- Additionally, the CK64 holds two 80-liter and two 50-liter tanks for black and gray water, respectively.
Designed with both performance and comfort in mind, the CK64 catamaran is a premium choice for those desiring a blend of luxury and practicality in marine travel.
Hull and Deck Construction:
- Hulls and superstructures constructed from aluminum alloy 5083 H111
- MIG-welded lined sheets with penetrant testing for integrity
- Alternately welded stringers, ribs, and floors
- Watertight bulkheads welded on both sides
- Integrated diesel and water tanks
- Non-ground welds on non-visible surfaces enhance weld resistance and nacelle safety
Appendages and Bar Systems:
- Profiled aluminum rudders
- Aluminum rudder stocks
- Marsili hydraulic tiller system
- Aluminum quillons
Insulation:
- Thermal and sound insulation using projected polyurethane foam above the waterline
- Engine and generator compartments feature reinforced insulation with rock wool, sound insulation foam, and perforated aluminum sheet
Paint Coatings and Finishes:
- International/awl grip mixed process
- Underwater parts: degreasing, pickling, 3-4 coats of epoxy primer totaling 200 μ, 1 coat of 50 μ epoxy vinyl, 2 coats of antifouling
- Topsides: degreasing, pickling, 50 μ epoxy primer, epoxy filler, polyurethane undercoat, and 3 polyester urethane finish coats
- Anti-slip epoxy paint on decks and traffic areas
- Teak steps on the rear cockpit, flybridge, and skirts, 8mm thick
Mast and Boom:
- 24m painted aluminum mast, Maréchal Z 1602
- 8.50m painted aluminum boom
- Navigation and anchor LED lights, wind vane, topmast light, and deck light
- Retractable mast rungs
- Ladder for coachroof access via forward cockpit on starboard
- Aluminum bowsprit
Standing and Furling Rigging:
- Sparcraft monostrand stainless steel rigging
- Profurl NC530 electric genoa furler
- Fixed staysail with carabiner
Sails:
- Elvstrom cruise dacron double ply supplier
- Fully battened mainsail of 120m² with 3 reefs and anti-chafe strips
- 130m² genoa with UV-protection band
- Total sail area of 250m² upwind and 320m² downwind
Safety and Protection:
- Stainless steel aft and bow pulpits and Ø26 stanchions
- Handrail behind aft cockpit seats
- Lifeline anchors in cockpits and on deck
- Welded aluminum toe rail
- 4mm double-level stainless steel die
- 85cm high chandeliers and balconies
- Wooden seats on front pulpit
- A door on each side
Cockpit and Aft Skirts:
- A vast aft cockpit shielded by the upper deck and equipped with lighting
- Seating with large storage compartments
- Teak flooring and steps
- Three benches featuring storage boxes
- Six combination stool/storage units
- Drink refrigerator and freezer
- Cockpit dining table
- Comfortable stainless steel swim ladder on the port skirt for man overboard situations
- Customizable aft cockpit upholstery for seats and backrests
- Customizable aft cockpit sundeck upholstery
- Complete aft cockpit enclosure
- Underwater lighting with high-intensity projectors at the stern
 Helm Station:
- Elevated helm station with a two-seater pilot seat
- Helming console equipped with a magnetic compass
- Console with cable-operated engine controls
- Raymarine Axiom 15 navigation unit, visible from the helm
- Composite steering wheel
- Electronic engine controls
- Remote control for engine and navigation systems for docking and anchoring
Main Features:
- Interior finishes made of veneered marine plywood sandwich and honeycomb (using NIDAPLAST with formica and glued edges on visible parts) for weight reduction
- Bathrooms finished in white laminate
- Kitchen countertops made of synthetic stone, akin to Corian
- Headroom of 2.15-2.25m in living quarters
- Safety handrails in descent areas
- Marine grade hardware
- Indirect and step lighting for ambiance and safety
Galley:
- U-shaped galley on starboard side with access to the aft cockpit via sliding window
- Double deep stainless steel sink with hot/cold water tap
- Synthetic stone counters
- Storage in cupboards and a storage column
- Professional 3-burner gas cooker (Burgundy Embassy)
- External venting extractor hood
- Large HAIER HB21F refrigerator/freezer, or similar model
- Dishwasher
Cabins:
- Four "Grand Comfort" cabins
- 150 x 200 cm double beds with dual-side access in aft cabins
- High-density mattresses on slatted bases
- Ample storage including large wardrobes, shelves, and drawers
- Office space, LED and ambient lighting, with two reading lights in each cabin
- 2.15 m headroom, large windows, deck hatch, and large mirror in each cabin
Bathrooms:
- One bathroom per cabin
- PLANUS electric toilet
- Basin set in counter with storage and hot/cold mixer tap
- Towel rack, mirror with LED lighting
- Port window in the hull, large deck hatch, and large windows
- Separate shower with plexiglass walls
Machinery:
- Twin 110 HP engines, optional SD4.140 TIC SCAM 3.8l
- Sea water filter, electric motor control
- Double cylinder hydraulic transmission Marsili
- Forced air engine compartment ventilation
- SCAN SDA10M 10Kva 380V generator and 280l/h Hydrotechnical watermaker
Electricity:
- 24V electrical system with a set of 1,600 Ah lead gel service batteries
- 4 x 140A lithium batteries
- 24V main panel with battery monitor, protection box
- Dual 190Ah alternators for lithium batteries
- Engine starter batteries with redundancy switch
- Numerous AC outlets and USB sockets throughout the vessel
- LED lighting both outside and inside, ambient, courtesy, and underwater lights
Onboard Comfort:
- Washer-dryer in starboard cockpit, extra cockpit refrigeration or storage
- Individual U Flex velair air conditioning 7000 BTU per cabin and compact systems in saloon
- Optional fuel heating
Sound and Electronics:
- Raymarine primary electronics supplier
- VHF with DSC, AIS700, i70s pack, AXIOM 12' touch screens, and wireless remote control
- Quantum Q24D Doppler radar, Realvision 3D probe, and backup autopilot system
- Marine stereo with Bluetooth, interior and exterior loudspeakers, radio pack
- Customized audio-video system in the saloon
